I hope you're ready to put your knowledge to the test with another scenario. In this situation, you're an account manager for a small marketing firm, SuperSauce Marketing. Your client is ABC Corporation, which is a Fortune 500 company. Your point of contact is a man named Mike. Over the past two years, you've done a lot of work for them, but they stopped paying their bills and now owe your firm more than $25,000. Every time you bring it up to Mike, he tells you that you'll get a lot of business in the future and that the money is on the way. Your boss has told you in no uncertain terms to straighten the account out. He wants you to get paid every penny your company has owed without running up any more delinquencies and not losing the contract with ABC Corporation. That's your first move.
